title: WSL and HyperV machine tests are failing but CI is passing
body:
### Issue Description

#20841 revealed that our WSL CI is broken ... there are failing tests and CI is passing.  https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4633577858531328?logs=main#L205 has details for example.

In looking at HyperV, the same is also true; failing tests and passing CI.

comments.body: Oof, this is pretty big. @ashley-cui @edsantiago @cevich PTAL

Weird. This is Windows, so I'm clueless, but here's my analysis:
- the script getting run is [contrib/cirrus/win-podman-machine-test.ps1](contrib/cirrus/win-podman-machine-test.ps1)
- Its last line is `.\winmake localmachine` and I see that echoed in the log, and what looks like it actually running
- the [end of the log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4633577858531328?logs=main#L1571) very clearly shows, multiple times, "fail" and "exit code = 1"
- from here it goes to [contrib/cirrus/win-sess-launch.ps1](contrib/cirrus/win-sess-launch.ps1), at the very bottom it says "Failure occurred, see above. Extra info:" and that appears in the log. But there's a `throw "wsl task failed on us!"` line, and that string does not appear in the log. 

Conclusion: the failure is happening in the middle of these three lines, `Receive-Job`: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/572a7692e3e38c4966d712f56dc9cb2c2e975968/contrib/cirrus/win-sess-launch.ps1#L64-L66

The string `Receive-Job` does not appear anywhere else in our source tree, so I'm guessing it's a Windows builtin. At this point, we need a Windows expert.
OK. For a plan moving forward: Can we get a branch where CI is set to fail when tests are bad, and iterate on that branch until things are green?
Yes, this is a dupe of #20548
